steps.title: Separate independent clauses
steps.content: The first clause says a subseasonal forecast predicts weather three to four weeks in advance. The second clause begins with its predictions and is also independent. These two clauses need a valid boundary between them.

steps.title: Use semicolon punctuation
steps.content: A uses only a comma, creating a comma splice. B leaves the independent clauses fused with no punctuation. D uses and without the needed comma and does not fit smoothly with therefore. The remaining punctuation correctly separates two independent clauses. The answer is C.
